title: Chýně

infobox.value: is a town in [Prague-West District](/wiki/Prague-West_District "Prague-West District") in the [Central Bohemian Region](/wiki/Central_Bohemian_Region "Central Bohemian Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 5,000 inhabitants.

**Chýně** is a town in [Prague-West District](/wiki/Prague-West_District "Prague-West District") in the [Central Bohemian Region](/wiki/Central_Bohemian_Region "Central Bohemian Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 5,000 inhabitants.

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The initial name of the settlement was probably Chajín. The name was derived from the personal name Chája, meaning "Chája's (court)". The name was gradually distorted to Chajn and then to Chejn. From the 19th century, the name Chýně is used.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Chýně is located about 5 kilometres (3 mi) west of Prague. It lies in an agricultural landscape of the [Prague Plateau](/wiki/Prague_Plateau "Prague Plateau").

The stream Litovický potok runs east along the northern border and supplies two small fishponds. The upper pond is called Bašta and is used for recreational purposes. The lower pond is called Strahovský rybník.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

The area of the town has been inhabited since prehistoric times. Iron furnaces that were found here are the oldest examples known in Bohemia and Moravia. The first written mention of Chýně is from 1273.[[3]](#cite_note-about-3)

The village used to belong to [Strahov Monastery](/wiki/Strahov_Monastery "Strahov Monastery") and until recently it was a small farming community. Proximity to Prague, the airport, and the motorway to Plzeň led in the 1990s to the development of some logistics and construction industries. The municipality has grown considerably with some large housing developments to the west, and it is losing some of its rural character.[[3]](#cite_note-about-3) In 2023, Chýně was promoted to a town.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/23/Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B%2C_obecn%C3%AD_%C3%BA%C5%99ad.JPG/250px-Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B%2C_obecn%C3%AD_%C3%BA%C5%99ad.JPG)](/wiki/File:Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B,_obecn%C3%AD_%C3%BA%C5%99ad.JPG)

Town hall

Chýně has a small industrial area in the east.

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Transport")]

Chýně is located on the railway line Prague–[Rudná](/wiki/Rudn%C3%A1_(Prague-West_District) "Rudná (Prague-West District)"). In addition to the *Chýně* train station, the town is aserved by the *Chýně jih* station, which is situated south of the town just beyond the municipal border.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ch%C3%BDn%C4%9B&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sights")]

There are no protected [cultural monuments](/wiki/Cultural_monument_(Czech_Republic) "Cultural monument (Czech Republic)") in the town.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
